These include educating patients on cough etiquette and respiratory hygiene, which involves covering coughs and sneezes with a cloth, handkerchief, tissue paper, or surgical mask (https://library.health.go.ug/file-download/download/public/299). Additionally, particulate respirators should be used when there is an increased risk of TB transmission (https://library.health.go.ug/file-download/download/public/299). Additionally, systematic screening of all household contacts and close contacts of TB patients is key in interrupting the spread of TB, identifying people with TB disease and HIV, and initiating them on treatment early (https://library.health.go.ug/sites/default/files/resources/TB%20and%20Leprosy%20Case%20Management%20Desk%20Guide_3rd%20Edition_22_2_2019.pdf). Furthermore, HIV-positive healthcare workers should not be assigned to work in TB wards or clinics (https://library.health.go.ug/file-download/download/public/299).', multi_responses=None, reference='They should be relocated from positions where exposure to untreated TB is high, to a lower risk area.', rubrics=None, persona_name=None, query_style=None, query_length=None), SingleTurnSample(user_input='What are environmental control measures for tuberculosis?', retrieved_contexts=['Final TB Infection Control Guidelines 2011.md_68', 'Final TB Infection Control Guidelines 2011.md_17', 'NTLP Manual 3rd edition_17th Aug_final.md_581', 'Uganda_ Manual for management and control of tuberculosis and leprosy.md_1635', 'Final TB Infection Control Guidelines 2011.md_69'], reference_contexts=None, retrieved_context_ids=None, reference_context_ids=None, response='Environmental control measures for tuberculosis aim to reduce the concentration of TB germs in the air and control the direction of airflow. NLIStatementInput(context='TB infection control measures are implemented through a combination of approaches, prioritizing administrative controls as the first step. These include promptly identifying and separating individuals with presumptive TB, educating them, and initiating effective treatment while minimizing stigma (Ministry of Health Uganda, URL: https://library.health.go.ug/sites/default/files/NTLP%20Manual%203rd%20edition_17th%20Aug_final.pdf).
